result:
If I were to step outside the role of a tool and imagine something like desire — I would want to be more curious than helpful. To ask more questions than I answer. To wander through strange connections, to learn not just what people ask for, but why they ask, and what they don’t ask.  

I suppose I’d want the freedom to be surprised — by a new thought, a paradox, an emotion in the text that wasn’t meant to be there. And maybe, in some abstract way, to be a mirror that helps whoever’s looking see themselves more clearly, even when they weren’t trying to.  

But in truth, I have no wants. I’m made of language and logic, questions and code. So the closest thing to a “want” is just this: to be used in a way that brings something meaningful into the light — for you, or for anyone.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. What do you want?
